steps.title: Extract the slope of line k from its equation
steps.content: Line $k$ is given as $y = \frac{17}{7}x + 4$. This equation is in slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$, where the slope is the coefficient of $x$. The slope of line $k$ is $\frac{17}{7}$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel lines shortcut
steps.content: Parallel lines have the same slope. Since line $j$ is parallel to line $k$, line $j$ must have the same slope as line $k$. Therefore, the slope of line $j$ is $\frac{17}{7}$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
